title: Tabs with Manual Activation Example — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Reference implementation of manual-activation tabs. Arrow keys move focus but do NOT activate. Enter/Space activates. Use when tab panels contain async-loaded content. Compare with auto-activation example.

title: Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Reference implementation for autocomplete combobox. aria-autocomplete='list', aria-activedescendant tracks active option. Down Arrow moves to list. Escape clears/closes. Essential for search inputs and select-with-search patterns.

title: Breadcrumb Example — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Live implementation. nav aria-label='Breadcrumb' wraps ol. aria-current='page' on last link. CSS-only separators via ::after or content. Accessible in JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver.

title: @storybook/addon-a11y — npm
insights: Current version 8.6.0. Wraps axe-core. Installation: npx storybook add @storybook/addon-a11y. Config in .storybook/preview.js. Works with Test addon for automated background checks.

title: Accessibility Testing with Storybook — Storybook Tutorials
insights: Full tutorial: install addon, configure axe rules, write stories, view panel results, integrate with CI. Shows how a11y gate in Storybook prevents regressions per component.

title: axe-core 4.5: First WCAG 2.2 Support — Deque Blog
insights: First version with WCAG 2.2 rules. Documents which of the 9 new SC are automatable. Shows how axe-core evolves to track standards updates. Key version for WCAG 2.2 adoption in CI pipelines.

title: ARIA — Accessibility — MDN Web Docs
insights: MDN's ARIA hub. Covers roles, states, properties, live regions, techniques. Key principle: semantic HTML before ARIA. ARIA on div does NOT make it keyboard-interactive. Foundational reference.

title: Accessibility in React — MDN Learn
insights: React-specific a11y guide: focus management after DOM updates, managing focus with refs/useEffect, keyboard trap in modals, ESLint jsx-a11y integration, routing focus management with react-router.

title: Accessibility — React Docs
insights: Official React a11y guidance: programmatic focus, fragment as grouping element, accessible forms, aria-* support, semantic HTML. Includes note on React-specific deviations (className, htmlFor).

title: Build an Accessible Modal with focus-trap-react — LogRocket
insights: focus-trap-react library wraps modal for keyboard trap. Must also add ARIA: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby. Return focus to trigger button on close. HTML dialog element showModal() as modern alternative.

title: Using Media Queries for Accessibility — MDN CSS
insights: Hub for all accessibility media queries: prefers-reduced-motion, prefers-color-scheme, prefers-contrast, forced-colors, inverted-colors. Multiple queries often used together for robust support.

title: prefers-reduced-motion: No-Motion-First Approach — Tatiana Mac
insights: Flips the pattern: no-motion as default, add animation in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference). More inclusive than adding @media reduce as an afterthought. Widely cited approach in a11y community.

title: The Complete Guide to ARIA Live Regions — A11Y Collective
insights: Comprehensive live regions guide. Covers all values, aria-atomic, aria-relevant, timing pitfalls. Key warning: inserting content into live region immediately at page load may not be announced. Pre-render container, inject message later.

title: w3c/aria-practices — GitHub (WAI-ARIA APG source)
insights: Source code for the APG. Active 2024 updates: forced-colors media query additions, radio group Enter key removal, combobox aria-selected alignment, scrollable listbox improvements. Task force weekly calls.

title: WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured: Complete Implementation Guide — AllAccessible
insights: Practical guide: CSS scroll-padding solution. scroll-padding-top = sticky header height. Conditional dismiss for cookie banners. scroll-margin alternative on individual elements.

title: WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) Implementation Guide — AllAccessible
insights: 24x24 CSS px minimum OR offset spacing. Inline text links exempt. Hit area != visual size. Best practice 44x44. CSS padding often the easiest fix. Mobile native guidelines (Apple 44pt, Material 48dp) stricter than WCAG AA.

title: WCAG 2.5.7 Dragging Movements Implementation Guide — AllAccessible
insights: Practical alternatives for dragging: numerical input next to slider, Up/Down keys for range, Delete button instead of drag-to-trash, explicit sort buttons instead of drag-to-reorder. All Level AA.

title: WebAIM Screen Reader Survey #10: Key Takeaways 2024 — Allyant
insights: Survey analysis: headings remain top navigation method. Only 34.6% report a11y improvement (declining trend). Importance of real-user testing beyond automated checks. Planning matrix for testing priorities.

title: Key Findings From WebAIM 2024 Screen Reader Survey — Colleen Gratzer / Medium
insights: Design-focused analysis of SR survey. Emphasis on headings, ARIA landmarks, form labels from SR users' perspective. Practical design implications.

title: prefers-reduced-motion — CSS-Tricks
insights: Comprehensive almanac entry. Syntax, values, real examples. Integration with GSAP and other animation libraries. System settings that trigger it (Windows Reduce Motion, macOS Reduce Motion, iOS, Android).

title: Lighthouse Accessibility Score — Chrome Developers
insights: Weighted axe-core score. Critical/Serious findings weigh more. score ≥ 90 recommended minimum in LHCI config. Does NOT mean WCAG conformant — only tests what axe-core can automate.

title: Dialog (Modal) Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby. Focus moves to dialog on open, returns to trigger on close. Escape closes. Tab cycles within dialog (focus trap). aria-describedby for dialog description. HTML dialog element is modern native alternative.

title: Kit d'audit RGAA — accessibilite.numerique.gouv.fr
insights: Official DINUM audit kit. Spreadsheet templates for RGAA 4.1 full audit (106 criteria) and simplified audit. Test methodology for each criterion. Essential for French public sector compliance.

title: Générateur de Déclaration d'Accessibilité — BetaGouv/DINUM
insights: Free tool from French government (BetaGouv) to generate the mandatory accessibility declaration (déclaration d'accessibilité) required under French law. Required for public sector and now extended to private sector.

title: RGAA: New Accessibility Rules Coming in 2025 — ATI4Group
insights: Analysis of RGAA evolution. Private sector obligations from June 2025 (EAA transposition). RGAA v5 planned end 2026. Key for planning 2025-2026 compliance roadmaps for French private companies.

title: WCAG 2.2 SC 3.3.7 Redundant Entry — Hong Kong Digital Policy Office
insights: Government implementation guide for SC 3.3.7. Auto-populate or present previously entered data as selectable option. Scope does not extend across sessions. Exception for security re-entry.

title: Sara Soueidan — Blog
insights: Top accessibility engineering blog. 80+ articles on HTML, CSS, SVG, ARIA, accessibility. Recent 2025: CSS carousels accessibility analysis, scroll-marker-group, CSS alt text syntax. Deep technical depth, no fluff.

title: WCAG 2.2 New Success Criteria Implementation Guide — TestParty
insights: Developer-focused implementation guide for all 9 new SC. Code examples for each criterion. Good companion to W3C understanding documents.

title: Manual vs Automated Accessibility Testing: A Strategic Comparison — TestParty
insights: Automated = 30-40% coverage, runs on every commit. Manual expert = monthly. User testing with disabled users = quarterly. Layered approach: testing pyramid for a11y. No single method catches all issues.

title: Listbox Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: role='listbox', role='option'. aria-selected tracks selection. Multi-select uses aria-multiselectable. Arrow keys navigate, Space selects. Different from select element — avoid unless native HTML can't serve the need.

title: Tree View Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: role='tree', role='treeitem', role='group' for sub-trees. Arrow keys: right expands, left collapses/moves up, down/up navigates. aria-expanded state. Complex pattern — use with care, simpler alternatives preferred.

title: Practical HTML, CSS, ARIA & JS Tips for Accessible Mega Menus — A11Y Collective
insights: Do NOT use role='menu'/role='menuitem' for navigation — hides links from link list shortcuts. Use nav+ul+li. aria-expanded on trigger. Click (not hover) to open. Escape closes. Return focus to trigger on close.

title: Understanding SC 2.3.3: Animation from Interactions — WAI/W3C
insights: AAA level. Parallax scrolling, page transitions, progress indicator animations — all can be disabled via prefers-reduced-motion. Not limited to vestibular; covers all motion sickness triggers.

title: Accessible Rich Internet Applications (WAI-ARIA) 1.2 — W3C
insights: Full ARIA 1.2 specification. Combobox pattern updated significantly from 1.1. New roles: comment, mark, suggestion, cell, row, rowgroup. Role taxonomy and inheritance model. ARIA 1.3 draft in development.

title: EN 301 549 Compliance — Deque
insights: Practical guide to EN 301 549 compliance using axe-core. EN 301 549 v3.2.1 includes WCAG 2.1 AA. v4.1.1 planned 2026 for WCAG 2.2 and EAA. Deque tools mapped to EN 301 549 requirements.

title: European Accessibility Act (EAA) Complete Compliance Guide 2025 — AllAccessible
insights: June 28 2025 deadline. EN 301 549 as conformance standard. WCAG 2.1 AA embedded. Spain: €1M max, Germany: €500k, France: €300k, Hungary: €1.26M. Exempts <10 employees companies. Pre-2025 archived content exempt.

title: European Accessibility Act & GDPR: What to Expect in 2025 — Quertum
insights: EAA mirrors GDPR enforcement model. Similar legislative structure. EAA seen as 'next GDPR' for scope and penalties. Privacy + accessibility intersection: inaccessible cookie banners create both GDPR and EAA violations simultaneously.

title: Protecting Persons with Disabilities in a Digitised World — Privacy International
insights: Disability+privacy intersection analysis. Digitization of social support = accessibility opportunity + privacy risk. Automated decision systems bias against disability. Data about disability status = sensitive personal data under GDPR Article 9.

title: European Accessibility Act Compliance: What's Next for GDPR — ADA Site Compliance
insights: Both EAA and GDPR require cookie consent to be accessible (readable, keyboard-navigable, sufficient contrast). Inaccessible consent UI = dual violation. Clear legal intersection for SaaS products in EU.

title: Headings — WAI Page Structure Tutorial
insights: Heading hierarchy as page outline. One H1 per page. No skipped levels. 67.5% of SR users navigate by headings (WebAIM SR survey). Visual styling must not drive heading choice. Heading = semantic level, not size.

title: Understanding WCAG 2.2 — WAI/W3C
insights: Official Understanding documents for all 87 WCAG 2.2 success criteria. Each page: intent, benefits, examples, techniques, failures. The definitive reference behind the spec text. Use for any ambiguous criterion.
